findings.problem: step_02 pins src/sysdiff.c at sha256 8df0bbfa93c53f01ef90551ce87d0bc6e552e424bfcc1656af0c02d4cd5ade11, but step_01's allowed_paths explicitly includes src/. Any successful C-source bugfix in step_01 changes src/sysdiff.c and produces a different hash, causing step_02's file_hash_matches check to fail and halt the run. The on_fail retry message misleads by stating the oracle changed outside allowed paths, when the change was a legitimate repair. The template cannot fix bugs in src/sysdiff.c — its primary use case — without the smoke gate blocking every run.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove the file_hash_matches entry for src/sysdiff.c from bugfix.yaml step_02. The oracle mechanism (scripts/smoke.sh, tests/test_sysdiff.sh, Makefile) is already protected by the three remaining pins; the step_01 allowed_paths constraint already limits what the bugfix worker can write. Pinning the subject under repair is counterproductive.

findings.problem: The smoke output path is hardcoded as artifacts/user-smoke/result.json with no __SLICE_NAME__ placeholder. Sibling templates use discriminated names (bugfix-result.json, onboarding-ci-bootstrap-result.json). The hardcoded path collides with the existing committed smoke artifact from run d3b2e8bc606c and provides no sentinel to alert a specializing author to rename it before launch, so evidence from prior runs can be silently overwritten.
findings.proposed_fix: Change the output path to artifacts/user-smoke/__SLICE_NAME__-result.json and update the matching file_exists validator to use the same placeholder, consistent with the naming convention in sibling templates.

findings.problem: The step_02_approve_contract human_approval step was removed. Step IDs jump from step_01 to step_03. Autonomous runs now proceed from contract authoring to planning and implementation with no human checkpoint on contract intent. The authoring_notes array does not flag this gap or advise specialists to consider re-inserting a human gate for high-stakes slices. Remaining safety controls catch implementation failures but not contract-intent failures.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a note to authoring_notes stating that the human_approval gate between step_01 and step_03 was intentionally removed for autonomous-mode runs, and that specialists should evaluate re-inserting a human_approval step after step_01_define_slice_contract before launching slices where contract correctness is high-stakes.

findings.location: step_02_user_smoke_gate, worker field; also onboarding_ci_bootstrap.yaml step_02 and repair_before_review_feature_delivery.yaml step_08
findings.problem: All three smoke gate steps use worker: smoke_runner. The smoke_runner harness does not appear in workers.allowed in .agent-orch/project.yaml (which lists fake_worker, codex_cli, pi_cli, claude_code, grok_cli). A strict-lint pass that validates step worker fields against the project registry would flag all three steps as referencing an unregistered harness. At runtime, Agent-Orch attempting to dispatch to an unregistered worker produces an opaque failure.
findings.proposed_fix: Either add smoke_runner to workers.allowed in .agent-orch/project.yaml, or replace worker: smoke_runner in all three smoke gate steps with a registered harness appropriate for running shell smoke commands.

checks_run.summary: The .agent-orch/ directory contains only YAML and the playbooks/ tree contains only JSON; no Python source files are present in either tree. compileall found nothing to compile and exited cleanly. No Python syntax errors were introduced by the repair.

strict-lint-compatibility.justification: All previously blocking placeholder clusters (__JUDGE_HARNESS__, per-step routing placeholders, execution block placeholders, and all-zeros sha256 sentinel values) were resolved. Top-level routing and roles blocks were added to all five templates; role fields were added to all steps. Remaining __PLACEHOLDER__ sentinels are intentional and documented in authoring_notes. F001 is the residual strict-lint blocker: the src/sysdiff.c pin in bugfix.yaml step_02 conflicts with step_01's allowed_paths, making any specialized launch for a C-source bugfix fail deterministically at the pin check. F004 is a new finding: smoke_runner is used as the worker in three smoke gate steps but is not registered in workers.allowed in .agent-orch/project.yaml, which may cause strict-lint or runtime dispatch failures.

autonomous-run-safety.justification: run_policy (on_step_failure: halt, require_validation_pass_to_advance: true) is correctly set across all five playbooks. All steps carry explicit role or worker bindings. Review steps in all three templates use the checks_run_match + review_verdict_clean pair with severity_threshold High, correctly enforcing route-back on High/Critical findings. F003 records the undocumented removal of the human_approval gate between contract authoring and autonomous implementation; remaining controls cover implementation correctness, not contract intent.

json-output-assumptions.justification: The json_schema + review_verdict_clean + checks_run_match triple-gate is consistently applied across all three new review steps. json_parse validators are present on all smoke artifact outputs. Review step missions explicitly instruct the worker to include only deterministic allowlisted commands in checks_run. F002 records the hardcoded artifact path in the main delivery template's smoke gate, which lacks a placeholder sentinel to force specialization and risks silently overwriting prior run evidence.

smoke-oracle-grounding.justification: All four oracle file hashes (scripts/smoke.sh, tests/test_sysdiff.sh, Makefile, src/sysdiff.c) were verified against the current working tree and match the pinned values. The onboarding_ci_bootstrap.yaml smoke gate is structurally sound because step_01 writes only to .github/ and docs/, leaving none of the pinned files reachable. The repair_before_review_feature_delivery.yaml step_08 creates a latent version of F001: it pins src/sysdiff.c while step_07 uses __PROJECT_SRC__/ as allowed_paths — when specialized to src/ for a sysdiff slice, the pin and the allowed write path conflict. F001 names bugfix.yaml as the concrete case where the conflict is certain rather than latent.

maintainability.justification: Addition of top-level roles blocks removes per-step routing duplication. markdown_headings_present gates with min_chars_under_heading replace bare file_exists checks in starter_proof.yaml, bugfix.yaml, and docs_only.yaml. Removal of the execution block from step_05_implement_slice eliminates an unresolvable placeholder cluster. F002 records naming inconsistency across smoke artifact paths that reduces composability and risks silent evidence collisions. F004 records a gap between the playbook worker declarations and the project worker registry that must be kept in sync manually.
